question: How is proinsulin processed to mature insulin?
options: Cleaved by furin in the cytoplasm
Cleaved by PC1/3 and PC2 in secretory granules
Cleaved by trypsin in the intestine
explanation: Proinsulin undergoes proteolytic processing by PC1/3 and PC2 in pancreatic beta cell secretory granules, removing the C-peptide to yield mature insulin.
